description_long: This object is a compact shotgun with a dark metal barrel and receiver, paired with a wooden fore-end and pistol grip. The barrel is relatively short, giving it a tactical, sawed-off appearance, and it features a tubular magazine beneath the main barrel. The receiver is robust and angular, with a visible lever-action mechanism below, and the trigger guard is large and rounded, accommodating the lever. The wooden grip and fore-end have a rich, dark brown finish, contrasting with the matte black metal components. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation, emphasizing function over form. The shotgun's compactness and lever-action suggest it is designed for close-quarters use, typical in urban environments.
description: A compact, lever-action shotgun with a short black barrel, tubular magazine, and dark wooden grip and fore-end. The design is utilitarian and robust, with a large rounded trigger guard and minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular barrel opening and tubular magazine, with the fore-end and grip barely visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the shotgun, highlighting the short barrel, tubular magazine, wooden fore-end, and lever-action mechanism.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the wooden grip and the back of the receiver, with the barrel and magazine aligned away from the viewer.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, showing the lever-action, wooden components, and the overall compact profile.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel with a tubular magazine beneath; no visible ribbing or venting; short barrel length.
attribute_trigger_guard_design: Large, rounded trigger guard integrated with the lever, with an elongated oval shape and no visible finger grooves or reinforcement.
